statement.markdown: Given a list of integers, implement a function to check if it is possible to obtain every element exactly twice by performing a sequence of push and pop operations from an initially empty stack. The function should return True if it is possible, and False otherwise.
statement.inputFormat: List of integers, where each integer appears at most twice.
statement.outputFormat: Boolean indicating whether it is possible to obtain every element exactly twice.
statement.notes: The function should handle lists with a single element, which can be obtained twice.

constraints.description: List of integers, where each integer appears at most twice

examples.explanation: It is possible to obtain every element exactly twice: Push 1 and pop 1, push 2 and pop 2, push 3 and pop 3, repeat with 1 and 2.
It is not possible to obtain every element exactly twice, as the stack does not contain enough elements.

hints.text: First hint text: Check the edge cases and the condition for popping from the stack.

editorial.title: Incorrect Stack Implementation: Miscounted Elements Editorial
editorial.intuition: The goal is to find a valid sequence of push and pop operations that obtain every element exactly twice.
editorial.bruteForce: Naive implementation involves trying all possible orders of push and pop operations.
editorial.betterSolution: A more efficient solution uses a stack and a count array to keep track of the remaining elements.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution employs a stack to store the current sequence of elements.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The proof of correctness involves showing that the algorithm correctly identifies whether it is possible to obtain every element exactly twice.
editorial.dryRun: Step-by-step example run using the optimal solution.
